case_title: BRENDA J. WORLEY VS. ROBERT TYSKO et al

docket_entries.description: ORDER TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA STATE AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. UNDER ROYAL GLOBE, ANY VIABLE COMMON LAW CAUSES OF ACTION SHOULD BE MAINTAINED IN A SEPARATE ACTION. THE CHALLENGED CONDUCT ALLEGED IS PART OF THE NORMAL CLAIM PROCESS. WITH RESPECT TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR FRAUD AND NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION, PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PLEAD RELIANCE AND AS TO THE LATTER CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF ALLEGES ONLY FUTURE EVENTS WHICH ARE NOT ACTIONABLE. THE CLAIM OF NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS FAILS AS A MATTER OF LAW. BURGESS V SUPERIOR COURT 2 CAL 4TH 1064 (1992). NO OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT HAS BEEN ALLEGED TO ESTABLISH INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO ALLEGE A BASIS FOR NEGLIGENCE PER SE AND THE FACTS ALLEGED FAIL TO ESTABLISH A DUTY TO PLAINTIFF. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA STATE AUTOMOBILE ASSOCIATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: OFF CALENDAR, INSUFFICIENT PROOF OF SERVICE PER C.C.P. SECTION 1005. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: CHRISTINA PAXTON, CSR #1558
